reflection:
woke at 3am again. that is the third early wake in a row, 4am two days back, 3:30 the night before, 3:00 yesterday. the marcus aurelius read in the middle of the night was actually a smart move, you tired the brain out enough to fall back asleep. but then the anxiety just hung around all day. walk, breakfast, duolingo, fish dinner, five episodes of young sherlock holmes with cosita, and still that buzz behind everything. you even called it yourself in the diary, 'i think i'm forgetting to breathe.'

the worry you wrote down is the same one as the day before. 'am i going to be like this in two weeks.' and right next to it you wrote, 'it's always a bit of the drama before, was bad last year actually.' that is the part to hold onto. you have been here, and it lifted.

your thought record was blank yesterday, intensity zero to zero, you didn't actually run it. that exact worry, 'i'll be like this in two weeks,' is the one to put in today with the evidence column. ten minutes, properly, not skimmed.

also, 1445 calories on 14,000 steps is light. try to eat a real dinner before 8pm tonight so the body has fuel through the night.

the fish was good, the movie was good, cosita is there. that counts. you are not as off-track as the anxiety is telling you.
opener: three early wakes in a row, anxiety hung around all day even with everything done right
focus_for_today: run the thought record on 'i'll be like this in two weeks' with the evidence column, properly
